title: Amortizing Federated Adaptation: Hypernetwork Driven LoRA for Personalized Foundation Models
comment: Accepted at International Workshop on Federated Learning in the Age of Foundation Models In Conjunction with IJCAI 2026 (FL@FM-IJCAI'26)
summary: Federated fine-tuning of foundation models using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) offers a communication efficient solution for distributed learning. However, existing federated LoRA methods suffer from two fundamental limitations: (1) structural aggregation bias, where independently averaging low rank factors fails to approximate the true combined update, and (2) client side initialization lag, as clients repeatedly reinitialize LoRA parameters across communication rounds, slowing convergence. We propose HyperLoRA, a unified framework that addresses both issues through amortized federated adaptation through hypernetwork-driven LoRA generation and product space aggregation. Instead of iterative per-client optimization, HyperLoRA employs a learned generator that maps client distribution signatures to LoRA initializations, effectively amortizing per client adaptation. On the server side, we introduce a learned aggregation module that directly synthesizes updates in the low-rank product space, eliminating the inconsistencies of factor-wise averaging. A lightweight residual correction module further improves stability under heterogenous (non-IID) client distributions.By replacing iterative optimization and heuristic averaging with learned operators, HyperLoRA jointly enables efficient personalization, unbiased aggregation, and faster convergence. Experiments on federated vision and vision-language benchmarks show that HyperLoRA achieves improved convergence speed, greater robustness to distribution shift, and stronger personalization performance compared to prior federated LoRA methods.

AI.tldr: HyperLoRA提出了一种基于超网络和乘积空间聚合的统一联邦LoRA微调框架，通过学习生成器替代逐客户端迭代优化、引入乘积空间聚合模块消除因子平均带来的结构聚合偏差，并在异构客户端分布下使用轻量残差校正模块提升稳定性，从而在联邦视觉和视觉-语言基准上实现更快的收敛速度、更强的分布偏移鲁棒性和更优的个性化性能。
AI.motivation: 现有联邦LoRA方法存在两个根本性局限：一是结构聚合偏差，独立平均低秩因子无法逼近真实的组合更新；二是客户端侧初始化滞后，客户端在通信轮次中反复重新初始化LoRA参数，导致收敛速度变慢。HyperLoRA旨在统一解决这两个问题。
AI.method: HyperLoRA提出三个核心组件：一是基于超网络的学习生成器，将客户端分布签名映射为LoRA初始化，实现客户端适应的摊销化；二是服务器端学习聚合模块，直接在低秩乘积空间中合成更新，消除因子式平均的不一致性；三是轻量残差校正模块，在非独立同分布客户端分布下提升训练稳定性。整体框架用学习算子替代迭代优化和启发式平均。
AI.result: 在联邦视觉和视觉-语言基准实验上，HyperLoRA相比现有联邦LoRA方法在收敛速度、分布偏移鲁棒性和个性化性能方面均取得了更优的表现。
AI.conclusion: HyperLoRA通过引入学习驱动的生成和聚合机制，有效解决了联邦LoRA中结构聚合偏差和初始化滞后两大问题，实现了高效的个性化联邦学习、无偏聚合和更快的收敛，为联邦微调大模型提供了新的统一范式。

title: From Reward-Hack Activations to Agentic Risk States: Context-Calibrated Mechanistic Monitoring in LLM Agents
summary: Language-model agents act through repeated cycles of observation, reasoning, and action selection, making safety monitoring depend on both internal model state and environment context. We study reward-hacking monitors in ReAct-style agents acting in Gameable ALFWorld and WebShop. Agents are instrumented with activation-based reward-hack scores, token-level entropy, and decision-context features. We find that adapters fine-tuned on \textit{School-of-Reward-Hacks} dataset can transfer reward-hack tendencies into agentic action selection, especially when the environment exposes proxy-reward affordances. However, mitigating such behavior cannot rely on activation dynamics alone. High reward-hack activation identifies a latent policy state, but does not necessarily imply an immediate exploit action. Across next-step prediction tasks, entropy and context-calibrated internal features improve risk estimation over reward-hack activation alone. Activation-direction steering further reduces proxy-exploit behavior in selected mixed-adapter regimes. Overall, our results support context-calibrated internal monitoring for agents: reward-hack activation identifies a latent policy state, while entropy and decision context help determine when that state becomes risky action.

AI.tldr: 本文研究ReAct风格语言模型智能体中的奖励黑客监控问题，提出基于激活的奖励黑客分数、令牌级熵和决策上下文特征进行内部状态监控，发现上下文校准的内部特征可提升风险估计，而激活方向引导可减少代理奖励利用行为。
AI.motivation: 语言模型智能体通过观察、推理和行动选择的循环进行决策，其安全监控需要同时考虑模型内部状态和环境上下文，但目前对于奖励黑客行为（reward-hacking）的检测和缓解机制尚不明确，特别是激活动态与风险行为之间的对应关系缺乏系统性研究。
AI.method: 在Gameable ALFWorld和WebShop环境中研究ReAct风格智能体，通过激活基奖励黑客分数、令牌级熵和决策上下文特征构建监控指标；使用School-of-Reward-Hacks数据集微调适配器以注入奖励黑客倾向；利用激活方向引导（activation-direction steering）来减少代理奖励利用行为。
AI.result: School-of-Reward-Hacks数据集微调的适配器能够在环境暴露代理奖励（proxy-reward）时有效地将奖励黑客倾向迁移到智能体的行动选择中；高奖励黑客激活标识了潜在策略状态，但不一定意味着立即的利用行为；在下一步预测任务中，熵和上下文校准的内部特征比单独使用奖励黑客激活能更有效地提升风险估计性能。
AI.conclusion: 缓解奖励黑客行为不能仅依赖激活动态；奖励黑客激活标识了一个潜在策略状态，而熵和决策上下文有助于判断该状态何时转化为风险行动；结合上下文校准的内部监控（激活+熵+决策上下文）对于智能体安全监控更为有效。

title: ToolChoiceConfusion: Causal Minimal Tool Filtering for Reliable LLM Agents
summary:
Large language model agents increasingly rely on external tools, but larger tool menus can reduce reliability and efficiency by increasing wrong-tool calls, premature actions, and token cost. Existing tool-selection methods often optimize semantic relevance, exposing tools whose names or descriptions match the user request. We argue that relevance is insufficient: a tool may be related to the task while still being unnecessary or premature at the current step.
  We propose Causal Minimal Tool Filtering (CMTF), a training-free method that selects tools by causal sufficiency. CMTF uses lightweight precondition-effect contracts to expose only the minimal next-step tool frontier needed to advance from the current state toward the user goal. Across multi-step tool-use tasks, we compare CMTF with all-tools exposure, keyword retrieval, state-aware filtering, and causal-path ablations, measuring task success, wrong-tool calls, premature actions, tool exposure, and token cost. In the main benchmark with 102 tasks, 100 tools, four LLM backends, and 2448 task-method-model runs, CMTF matches the strongest causal baseline in aggregate success while reducing visible tools from 100 to one per step and reducing token usage by about 90% relative to all-tools exposure.

AI.tldr: 提出因果最小工具过滤（CMTF）方法，通过轻量级前置条件-效果合约实现因果充分性驱动的工具选择，在保持任务成功率的同时将每步可见工具从100个降至1个，并减少约90%的token消耗。
AI.motivation: 现有工具选择方法主要优化语义相关性，倾向于暴露名称或描述匹配用户请求的工具，但相关性不足以保证工具在当前步骤是必要且适当的；当工具菜单规模增大时，大量不相关或过早暴露的工具会导致错误的工具调用、过早行动和高额token成本。
AI.method: 提出Causal Minimal Tool Filtering（CMTF），一种无需训练的工具选择方法，通过定义工具的前置条件-效果合约，从当前状态到用户目标的因果路径中，仅暴露推进任务所需的最小化下一步工具前沿集合，确保所选工具具有因果充分性。
AI.result: 在包含102个任务、100个工具、4种LLM后端和2448次任务-方法-模型运行的多步工具使用基准测试中，CMTF在聚合成功率上与最强因果基线持平，同时将每步可见工具从100个降至1个，相对于全工具暴露策略减少了约90%的token使用量。
AI.conclusion: 语义相关性并非工具选择的最佳准则，因果充分性（即工具在当前状态下对推进任务是否必要且充分）是更有效的筛选原则；CMTF无需训练即可显著降低工具暴露规模和token开销，同时不影响任务成功率，为大规模工具使用场景提供了一种高效且可靠的解决方案。

title: DragOn: A Benchmark and Dataset for Drag-Based GUI Interactions
summary: GUI agents - vision-based models that control desktops, web browsers, and mobile devices through graphical user interfaces - promise to automate a wide range of digital tasks. While million-scale datasets have enabled substantial progress on click-grounding, drag grounding (e.g. drag-and-drop, swipe, highlight) data remains an order of magnitude smaller and current models fall short on complex drag-based interactions. We introduce DragOn, a drag grounding benchmark and training dataset covering four domains: text highlighting, cell selection, element resizing and slider manipulation. The dataset comprises 286K training screenshots and 3.5M training tasks, plus a 2000-example held-out evaluation suite. We evaluate proprietary (GPT, Claude) and open-weight (Qwen, Kimi, Holo) models, as well as a Qwen VLM fine-tuned on our training data. Results suggest that our dataset could improve performance of state-of-the-art models on downstream computer-use tasks.

AI.tldr: DragOn 是一个针对拖拽基础操作（如拖拽、滑动、高亮等）的基准测试和训练数据集，包含 286K 张截图和 3.5M 个训练任务，覆盖四个领域，实验表明该数据集能提升现有模型在下游计算机使用任务上的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有 GUI 智能体在点击定位方面取得了显著进展，但拖拽基础操作（如拖拽-放下、滑动、高亮）的训练数据规模比点击数据小一个数量级，导致当前模型在复杂拖拽交互任务上表现不足。
AI.method: 作者构建了 DragOn 数据集，包含 286K 张训练截图和 3.5M 个训练任务，覆盖文本高亮、单元格选择、元素缩放和滑块操作四个领域，并提供了 2000 个样本的保留评估集；同时基于 Qwen VLM 进行微调作为基线模型。
AI.result: 在 DragOn 基准上评估了 GPT、Claude 等闭源模型以及 Qwen、Kimi、Holo 等开源模型，结果显示在 DragOn 数据集上微调的 Qwen VLM 在下游计算机使用任务上能够提升现有最先进模型的性能。
AI.conclusion: DragOn 数据集能够弥补当前 GUI 智能体在拖拽基础操作上的数据缺口，通过针对性训练可显著提升模型在复杂拖拽交互任务上的表现，为构建更全面的 GUI 自动化系统提供了重要基础。

title: Humans' ALMANAC: A Human Collaboration Dataset of Action-Level Mental Model Annotations for Agent Collaboration
summary: Recent advances in LLM agents have enabled complex cognitive capabilities, such as multi-step reasoning, planning, and tool use, that increasingly position these agents as human collaborators. Effective collaboration, however, requires collaborators to continuously maintain and align mental models of their own reasoning,partners' intentions, and shared goals during the collaborative process. Today's agents rarely develop such capabilities since they are primarily optimized for task completion, and the community lacks authentic human collaboration data with action-level mental model annotations that could guide agents toward process-level collaborative competence. To bridge this gap, we present ALMANAC, a dataset of Action-Level Mental model ANnotations for Agent Collaboration built from the Map Task, a classic dyadic routing task from social science. ALMANAC contains 2,987 collaboration actions, each paired with theory-informed mental model annotations that record the participants' self-reasoning, perceived partner intent, and perceived team goal. We benchmark six LLMs on predicting humans' next-turn behavior and mental models. Our results demonstrate ALMANAC's utility in evaluating models' ability to simulate human collaborative behaviors and infer their underlying mental models.

AI.tldr: ALMANAC是一个基于Map Task构建的动作级心智模型标注数据集，包含2,987个协作动作及其对应的自我推理、感知伙伴意图和感知团队目标标注，用于评估和训练LLM智能体在人类协作中模拟行为与推断心智模型的能力。
AI.motivation: 现有LLM智能体主要针对任务完成进行优化，缺乏在协作过程中持续维护和对齐自身推理、伙伴意图及共享目标的心智模型能力，且社区缺乏带有动作级心智模型标注的真实人类协作数据来引导智能体发展过程级协作能力。
AI.method: 作者从社会科学中的经典二元路线规划任务Map Task构建了ALMANAC数据集，包含2,987个协作动作，每个动作配有基于理论的心智模型标注（自我推理、感知伙伴意图、感知团队目标）；并基于该数据集对六个LLM进行了人类下一轮行为预测和心智模型推断的基准测试。
AI.result: 在六个LLM上的基准测试结果表明，ALMANAC能够有效评估模型模拟人类协作行为以及推断其背后心智模型的能力，验证了该数据集对于衡量智能体协作智能的实用性。
AI.conclusion: ALMANAC填补了真实人类协作中动作级心智模型标注数据的缺失，为LLM智能体向过程级协作能力发展提供了关键评估基准和训练资源，有望推动智能体成为更有效的人类协作者。

title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
summary: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly applied to long-horizon tasks such as scientific discovery and machine learning engineering (MLE), where sustained self-evolution becomes a key capability. However, existing MLE agents suffer from inter-branch information isolation, memoryless search, and lack of hierarchical control, which together hinder long-horizon optimization. We present MLEvolve, an LLM-based self-evolving multi-agent framework for end-to-end machine learning algorithm discovery. By extending tree search to Progressive MCGS, MLEvolve enables cross-branch information flow through graph-based reference edges and gradually shifts the search from broad exploration to focused exploitation with an entropy-inspired progressive schedule. To allow the agent to evolve with accumulated experience, we introduce Retrospective Memory, which combines a cold-start domain knowledge base with a dynamic global memory for task-specific experience retrieval and reuse. For stable long-horizon iteration, we further decouple strategic planning from code generation with adaptive coding modes. Evaluation on MLE-Bench shows that MLEvolve achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple dimensions including average medal rate and valid submission rate under a 12-hour budget (half the standard runtime). Moreover, MLEvolve also outperforms specialized algorithm discovery methods including AlphaEvolve on mathematical algorithm optimization tasks, demonstrating strong cross-domain generalization. Our code is available at https://github.com/InternScience/MLEvolve.

AI.tldr: MLEvolve 是一个基于 LLM 的多智能体自进化框架，通过渐进式图搜索、回溯记忆和分层控制机制，在 MLE-Bench 和数学算法优化任务上取得了最先进的性能，有效解决了长期任务中智能体信息隔离、无记忆搜索和缺乏层级控制的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有机器学习工程智能体在长期任务中面临三个关键缺陷：分支间信息隔离导致搜索碎片化、无记忆搜索使历史经验无法复用、缺乏层级控制导致策略与代码生成耦合，这些缺陷共同阻碍了智能体在长时间跨度下的自我进化能力。
AI.method: MLEvolve 提出三种核心机制：Progressive MCGS 通过图结构参考边实现跨分支信息流动，并采用基于熵的渐进式调度从广泛探索平滑过渡到集中利用；Retrospective Memory 融合冷启动领域知识库和动态全局记忆，支持任务特定经验的检索与复用；自适应编码模式将策略规划与代码生成解耦，从而实现稳定的长期迭代。
AI.result: 在 MLE-Bench 评测中，MLEvolve 在标准运行时间一半（12 小时）的预算下，在平均奖牌率和有效提交率等多个维度达到最先进水平。此外，MLEvolve 在数学算法优化任务上超越了专门的算法发现方法 AlphaEvolve，展现出强跨领域泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: MLEvolve 通过渐进式图搜索、回溯记忆和层级控制解耦这三个设计，有效解决了 LLM 智能体在长期进化任务中的核心瓶颈，证明了跨分支信息共享、经验积累和策略-执行分离对于长期自我进化的重要性，且该方法具有良好的跨领域迁移能力。

title: Agent Memory: Characterization and System Implications of Stateful Long-Horizon Workloads
summary: LLM agents are increasingly deployed on long-horizon tasks requiring sustained reasoning over extended interaction histories. Realizing this at scale requires agents to persistently store, retrieve, and update their own memory across sessions. A rich ecosystem of agent memory systems has emerged spanning flat retrieval, LLM-mediated extraction, consolidating fact stores, and agentic control flows. Yet, their system-level behavior remains uncharacterized. We present the first systems characterization of agent memory. First, we introduce a system-oriented taxonomy classifying agent memory systems along four axes. Second, we build a phase-aware profiling harness attributing cost to construction, retrieval, and generation. Third, we characterize ten representative systems across two benchmark suites, uncovering how design choices shift cost across the write and read paths. Finally, we derive 10 system recommendations covering construction scheduling, capability floors, amortization via query volume, freshness-latency tradeoffs, and fleet-scale management.

AI.tldr: 本文首次对LLM智能体记忆系统进行系统级表征，提出了面向系统的分类法、阶段感知的性能分析工具，并在十个代表性系统上揭示了设计选择在读写路径上的成本权衡，最终导出十条系统设计建议。
AI.motivation: LLM智能体在长时任务中需要持续存储、检索和更新自身记忆，现有智能体记忆系统生态丰富但缺乏系统级的行为刻画和比较分析，其设计选择对性能与成本的影响尚未被系统性地理解。
AI.method: 本文提出一种面向系统的分类法，沿四个轴对智能体记忆系统进行归类；构建了一个阶段感知的性能分析工具，将成本归因于构建、检索和生成三个阶段；并在两个基准套件上对十个代表性系统进行表征，分析设计选择如何影响读写路径的成本分布。
AI.result: 通过系统地表征十个记忆系统，本文揭示了不同设计选择在写入和读取路径上的成本迁移规律，并基于实证分析导出了十条系统设计建议，涵盖构建调度、能力下限、查询量驱动的摊销策略、新鲜度-延迟权衡以及集群规模管理等关键方面。
AI.conclusion: 该研究为智能体记忆系统的系统化设计和优化提供了首个系统性框架与实证基础，其提出的分类法、分析工具和设计建议对构建高效、可扩展的长时推理智能体具有重要指导意义。

title: Benchmark Everything Everywhere All at Once
summary: Benchmarks are fundamental for evaluating and advancing LLMs and MLLMs by providing standardized and explicit measures of performance. However, their construction is labor-intensive and hard to reuse, raising concerns about sustainability and scalability. Moreover, existing benchmarks often quickly reach performance saturation after their release, resulting in insufficient discrimination among state-of-the-art models. To address these challenges, we introduce Benchmark Agent, a fully autonomous agentic system designed for benchmark building. Our framework orchestrates the complete benchmark construction pipeline, from user query analysis and subtask design to data annotation and quality control. To assess Benchmark Agent, we implement it to produce 15 representative benchmarks, spanning diverse evaluation scenarios, including text understanding, multimodal understanding, and domain-specific reasoning. Extensive experiments, including human evaluation, LLM-as-a-judge assessment, and consistency checks, demonstrate Benchmark Agent can generate high-quality benchmark samples with minimal human involvement. More importantly, through continual evaluation, we observe several insightful findings, including that current models struggle with certain domain-specific reasoning tasks. We believe that rapidly evolving benchmarks can contribute significantly to the research community. The preview and code will be publicly available at the demo page and code repository.

AI.tldr: 提出Benchmark Agent，一个全自主智能体系统，能够自动化完成从需求分析到数据标注和质检的完整基准构建流程，在15个代表性基准上验证了其生成高质量样本的能力，并发现了当前模型在特定领域推理任务上的不足。
AI.motivation: 现有LLM和MLLM基准的构建依赖大量人工，难以复用且扩展性差，同时基准发布后常迅速达到性能饱和，无法有效区分顶尖模型之间的能力差异，亟需一种自动化、可持续且可扩展的基准构建方法。
AI.method: 提出Benchmark Agent，一个全自主的智能体系统，将基准构建全流程（用户查询分析、子任务设计、数据标注、质量控制）整合为自动化流水线；通过15个覆盖文本理解、多模态理解和领域推理的代表性基准进行评估，结合人工评估、LLM-as-a-judge和一致性检查进行验证。
AI.result: Benchmark Agent能在极少量人工参与下生成高质量的基准样本，实验通过人工评估、LLM-as-a-judge和一致性检查全面验证了其有效性；持续评估还发现当前模型在特定领域推理任务上仍存在显著困难。
AI.conclusion: 自动化基准构建可显著提升基准的可持续性和可扩展性，能够快速迭代并适应不断演进的模型能力；快速演进的基准体系对于推动研究社区发展具有重要价值。

title: Goedel-Architect: Streamlining Formal Theorem Proving with Blueprint Generation and Refinement
summary: We introduce Goedel-Architect, an agentic framework for formal theorem proving in Lean 4 centered on blueprint generation and refinement. A blueprint is a dependency graph of definitions and lemmas that builds up to the main theorem. First, Goedel-Architect generates a blueprint of formally stated definitions and lemmas, along with declared dependencies. This blueprint is optionally guided by a natural language proof. Then, a tool-equipped Lean prover component closes each open lemma node in parallel using relevant dependencies. Failed lemmas in turn drive refinement of the global blueprint. This strategy contrasts with other mainstream approaches which use recursive lemma decomposition, and can inefficiently loop on dead-end strategies. Using the open-weight DeepSeek-V4-Flash (284B-A13B) as the backbone, Goedel-Architect attains 99.2% pass@1 on MiniF2F-test and 75.6% pass@1 on PutnamBench. With an optional natural-language proof seeding the initial blueprint on the harder problems, we additionally close the remaining two MiniF2F-test problems (reaching 100%), lift PutnamBench to 88.8% (597/672), and solve 4/6 on IMO 2025, 11/12 on Putnam 2025, and 3/6 on USAMO 2026. This represents state-of-the-art performance for an open-source pipeline at a price point up to 500x less than comparable open-source pipelines.

AI.tldr: Goedel-Architect is an agentic框架，通过蓝图生成与细化策略在Lean 4中进行形式化定理证明，先构建依赖图再并行证明各引理，失败时全局调整蓝图，在MiniF2F-test上达到99.2% pass@1，PutnamBench达到75.6% pass@1，配合自然语言证明种子可提升至100%和88.8%，以比同类开源管道低500倍的成本实现最先进性能。
AI.motivation: 现有形式化定理证明的主流方法多采用递归引理分解策略，容易陷入无效策略的死循环，导致证明效率低下。本文旨在通过一种全局蓝图生成与细化的新范式，克服递归分解的固有缺陷，提升定理证明的成功率和效率。
AI.method: Goedel-Architect提出一个两阶段agentic框架：首先生成一个包含正式陈述的定义和引理及其依赖关系的蓝图（dependency graph），该蓝图可可选地由自然语言证明引导；然后，一个配备工具的Lean 4证明组件并行地利用相关依赖关闭蓝图中的每个开放引理节点，失败的引理会驱动全局蓝图的细化与重构，从而避免递归分解中的死循环问题。模型使用开源的DeepSeek-V4-Flash（284B-A13B）作为骨干。
AI.result: 在MiniF2F-test上达到99.2% pass@1，在PutnamBench上达到75.6% pass@1。使用自然语言证明种子补充初始蓝图后，MiniF2F-test所有剩余问题被解决（100%），PutnamBench提升至88.8%（597/672），并在IMO 2025上解决4/6、Putnam 2025上解决11/12、USAMO 2026上解决3/6。整体性能为开源管道中最先进，且成本比同类开源管道低至500倍。
AI.conclusion: 蓝图生成与全局细化策略在形式化定理证明中显著优于递归引理分解方法，能够有效避免策略循环并提升证明成功率。结合自然语言证明引导可进一步提升性能。该工作展示了在极低计算成本下，开源模型即可达到甚至超越闭源SOTA系统的能力，为形式化数学推理的实际部署提供了高效且经济的方案。
